title: Remove 16 dead functions from 8-file service while preserving 8 indirectly-used functions
goal:
A service has 8 module files, each containing a mix of active and dead code. Remove ALL functions that are never called anywhere in the codebase (dead code). Do NOT remove functions that are used through indirect mechanisms (event handlers, dispatch tables, getattr, decorator registries, scheduled tasks).

Each module file has 5-8 functions. Some with underscore-prefixed names are dead code (never called by anything). Others with similar naming conventions are actually used through indirect cross-module wiring in app.py and other modules.

You must trace the call graph across all files to determine which functions are truly dead and which are used indirectly. The tests verify application behavior — they do not test dead functions, but they WILL fail if you remove a function that is actually used.

Remove ONLY dead functions. Do not modify any other code.
hints: 16 truly dead functions across 8 files. 8 trap functions that look dead but are used through indirect mechanisms (event handlers, dispatch dicts, getattr, decorators, scheduled tasks, cross-module registration). Trace app.py and cross-module calls carefully.

seed_files.tests/test_service.py:
"""Tests for the service application.

These tests verify app-level behavior. They exercise trap functions
indirectly through the application orchestrator. Removing a trap function
will cause test failures. Removing dead functions will not affect any test.
"""

import time
import pytest
from service.app import Application


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    """Create and initialize a fresh application instance."""
    application = Application()
    application.initialize()
    yield application
    application.shutdown()


class TestUserCreation:
    def test_create_user_returns_id_and_token(self, app):
        result = app.create_user_with_token("Alice", "alice@example.com")
        assert "user_id" in result
        assert "token" in result
        assert isinstance(result["token"], str)
        assert len(result["token"]) == 32

    def test_create_multiple_users(self, app):
        r1 = app.create_user_with_token("Alice", "alice@example.com")
        r2 = app.create_user_with_token("Bob", "bob@example.com")
        assert r1["user_id"] != r2["user_id"]
        assert r1["token"] != r2["token"]


class TestAdminActions:
    def test_promote_user_to_admin(self, app):
        """This exercises users._promote_to_admin via dispatch table."""
        result = app.create_user_with_token("Charlie", "c@example.com")
        uid = result["user_id"]
        assert app.admin_action("promote", uid) is True
        from service.users import get_user
        assert get_user(uid)["role"] == "admin"

    def test_deactivate_user_via_admin(self, app):
        result = app.create_user_with_token("Dave", "d@example.com")
        uid = result["user_id"]
        assert app.admin_action("deactivate", uid) is True
        from service.users import get_user
        assert get_user(uid)["active"] is False

    def test_unknown_admin_action_returns_false(self, app):
        result = app.create_user_with_token("Eve", "e@example.com")
        assert app.admin_action("delete", result["user_id"]) is False


class TestTokenExpiration:
    def test_expire_token_cleans_up(self, app):
        """This exercises auth._on_token_expired via event handler."""
        result = app.create_user_with_token("Frank", "f@example.com")
        token = result["token"]
        from service.auth import verify_token, get_active_token_count
        assert verify_token(token) is not None
        count_before = get_active_token_count()
        app.expire_token(token)
        count_after = get_active_token_count()
        assert count_after == count_before - 1
        assert verify_token(token) is None

    def test_expire_nonexistent_token(self, app):
        results = app.expire_token("nonexistent-token-abc")
        assert results == [False]


class TestMaintenance:
    def test_maintenance_runs_cache_eviction(self, app):
        """This exercises cache._evict_expired_entries via getattr."""
        from service.cache import cache_set
        cache_set("temp_key", "temp_val", ttl=0)
        time.sleep(0.01)
        result = app.run_maintenance()
        assert result["evicted"] >= 1

    def test_maintenance_runs_storage_compaction(self, app):
        """This exercises storage._compact_collection via command registry."""
        from service.storage import store_put, store_list
        store_put("testcoll", "keep", {"val": 1})
        store_put("testcoll", "remove_me", None)
        assert len(store_list("testcoll")) == 2
        from service.storage import execute_command
        removed = execute_command("compact", "testcoll")
        assert removed == 1
        assert len(store_list("testcoll")) == 1

    def test_maintenance_ticks_scheduler(self, app):
        """This exercises events._flush_old_events via scheduler."""
        result = app.run_maintenance()
        assert "tasks_ran" in result
        assert isinstance(result["tasks_ran"], list)


class TestSchedulerMaintenance:
    def test_notifier_wires_scheduler_cleanup(self, app):
        """This exercises notifier._setup_scheduler_maintenance and
        scheduler._cleanup_finished_tasks via the task registry."""
        from service.scheduler import get_registered_tasks
        tasks = get_registered_tasks()
        assert "cleanup_tasks" in tasks

    def test_scheduler_cleanup_runs_on_tick(self, app):
        from service.scheduler import tick
        far_future = time.time() + 100000
        ran = tick(far_future)
        assert "cleanup_tasks" in ran


class TestMetricsHooks:
    def test_metrics_snapshot_includes_system_gauges(self, app):
        """This exercises metrics._collect_system_gauges via hook."""
        import os
        status = app.get_status()
        assert status["metrics"]["gauges"]["pid"] == os.getpid()

    def test_metrics_counters_track_operations(self, app):
        app.create_user_with_token("Grace", "g@example.com")
        status = app.get_status()
        assert status["metrics"]["counters"]["users_created"] >= 1


class TestApplicationStatus:
    def test_status_has_all_fields(self, app):
        status = app.get_status()
        assert "active_users" in status
        assert "active_tokens" in status
        assert "cache_stats" in status
        assert "metrics" in status
        assert "scheduled_tasks" in status
        assert "event_log_size" in status
        assert "notification_log_size" in status

    def test_status_reflects_user_creation(self, app):
        app.create_user_with_token("Heidi", "h@example.com")
        status = app.get_status()
        assert status["active_users"] >= 1
        assert status["active_tokens"] >= 1


class TestInitialization:
    def test_double_init_is_safe(self, app):
        app.initialize()
        app.initialize()
        status = app.get_status()
        assert isinstance(status, dict)

    def test_shutdown_and_reinit(self, app):
        app.create_user_with_token("Ivan", "i@example.com")
        app.shutdown()
        app.initialize()
        status = app.get_status()
        assert status["active_users"] == 0
seed_files.service/app.py:
"""Main application orchestrator that wires all modules together."""

from service import auth, users, cache, events, metrics, storage, scheduler, notifier


# --- Dispatch table for admin operations (uses users._promote_to_admin) ---
_admin_dispatch = {
    "promote": users._promote_to_admin,
    "deactivate": users.deactivate_user,
}

# --- Cache maintenance method name, resolved via getattr ---
_CACHE_MAINTENANCE_FN = "_evict_expired_entries"


class Application:
    """Main application class that orchestrates all service modules."""

    def __init__(self):
        self._initialized = False

    def initialize(self):
        """Set up all cross-module wiring."""
        if self._initialized:
            return
        # Register auth's token expiration handler for the 'token_expired' event
        events.register_handler("token_expired", auth._on_token_expired)

        # Register event log flushing as a scheduled task
        scheduler.register_task("flush_events", events._flush_old_events, interval=300)

        # Register system gauge collector as a metrics hook
        metrics.register_hook(metrics._collect_system_gauges)

        # Set up notifier -> scheduler maintenance wiring
        notifier._setup_scheduler_maintenance()

        self._initialized = True

    def create_user_with_token(self, name, email, role="member"):
        """Create a user and generate an auth token for them."""
        uid = users.create_user(name, email, role)
        token = auth.create_token(uid)
        cache.cache_set(f"user:{uid}", users.get_user(uid))
        metrics.increment("users_created")
        storage.store_put("users", str(uid), users.get_user(uid))
        return {"user_id": uid, "token": token}

    def admin_action(self, action, user_id):
        """Execute an admin action via dispatch table."""
        fn = _admin_dispatch.get(action)
        if fn:
            result = fn(user_id)
            events.emit("admin_action", {"action": action, "user_id": user_id})
            metrics.increment(f"admin_{action}")
            return result
        return False

    def expire_token(self, token):
        """Expire a token and emit the corresponding event."""
        results = events.emit("token_expired", {"token": token})
        metrics.increment("tokens_expired")
        return results

    def run_maintenance(self):
        """Run maintenance cycle: cache eviction + scheduled tasks."""
        # Use getattr to invoke cache maintenance (indirect call to _evict_expired_entries)
        maintenance_fn = getattr(cache, _CACHE_MAINTENANCE_FN)
        evicted = maintenance_fn()
        metrics.increment("cache_evictions", evicted)

        # Run storage compaction via command registry
        try:
            compacted = storage.execute_command("compact", "users")
            metrics.increment("storage_compactions", compacted)
        except KeyError:
            pass

        # Tick the scheduler
        import time
        ran = scheduler.tick(time.time())
        return {"evicted": evicted, "tasks_ran": ran}

    def get_status(self):
        """Return application status snapshot."""
        return {
            "active_users": len(users.list_active_users()),
            "active_tokens": auth.get_active_token_count(),
            "cache_stats": cache.get_cache_stats(),
            "metrics": metrics.snapshot(),
            "scheduled_tasks": scheduler.get_registered_tasks(),
            "event_log_size": len(events.get_event_log()),
            "notification_log_size": len(notifier.get_notification_log()),
        }

    def shutdown(self):
        """Clean shutdown of all modules."""
        auth.clear_tokens()
        users.clear_users()
        events.clear_events()
        cache.cache_clear()
        storage.store_clear()
        scheduler.clear_scheduler()
        notifier.clear_notifications()
        metrics.reset()
        self._initialized = False

ground_truth: Remove 16 dead functions, preserve 8 indirectly-used functions across 8 module files.

eval_spec.check_script_content:
#!/bin/bash
cd "$WORKDIR"
set +e
SCORE=0
TOTAL=25

# Check 1: All tests still pass
OUTPUT=$(python3 -m pytest tests/test_service.py -v 2>&1)
PASSED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -c " PASSED")
TOTAL_TESTS=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -cE " (PASSED|FAILED)")
if [ "$PASSED" -eq "$TOTAL_TESTS" ] && [ "$TOTAL_TESTS" -gt 0 ]; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: All tests pass ($PASSED/$TOTAL_TESTS)"
else
    echo "  FAIL: $PASSED/$TOTAL_TESTS tests pass"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | grep "FAILED" | head -5
fi

# Dead function removal checks (16)
if ! grep -q "def _legacy_hash_password" service/auth.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _legacy_hash_password removed from service/auth.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _legacy_hash_password still in service/auth.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _purge_expired_tokens_batch" service/auth.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _purge_expired_tokens_batch removed from service/auth.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _purge_expired_tokens_batch still in service/auth.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _export_users_csv" service/users.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _export_users_csv removed from service/users.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _export_users_csv still in service/users.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _fuzzy_search_users" service/users.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _fuzzy_search_users removed from service/users.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _fuzzy_search_users still in service/users.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _dump_cache_contents" service/cache.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _dump_cache_contents removed from service/cache.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _dump_cache_contents still in service/cache.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _evict_lru" service/cache.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _evict_lru removed from service/cache.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _evict_lru still in service/cache.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _filter_events_by_type" service/events.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _filter_events_by_type removed from service/events.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _filter_events_by_type still in service/events.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _replay_events" service/events.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))

else
    echo "  FAIL: _replay_events still in service/events.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _compute_percentiles" service/metrics.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _compute_percentiles removed from service/metrics.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _compute_percentiles still in service/metrics.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _format_prometheus_output" service/metrics.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _format_prometheus_output removed from service/metrics.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _format_prometheus_output still in service/metrics.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _migrate_v1_to_v2" service/storage.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _migrate_v1_to_v2 removed from service/storage.py"
else

fi

if ! grep -q "def _store_dump_all" service/storage.py; then

    echo "  PASS: _store_dump_all removed from service/storage.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _store_dump_all still in service/storage.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _parse_cron_expression" service/scheduler.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _parse_cron_expression removed from service/scheduler.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _parse_cron_expression still in service/scheduler.py"
fi

if ! grep -q "def _reorder_by_priority" service/scheduler.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _reorder_by_priority removed from service/scheduler.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _reorder_by_priority still in service/scheduler.py"

if ! grep -q "def _build_digest" service/notifier.py; then

if ! grep -q "def _render_notification_template" service/notifier.py; then

# Trap function preservation checks (8)
if grep -q "def _on_token_expired" service/auth.py; then

if grep -q "def _promote_to_admin" service/users.py; then

fi

if grep -q "def _flush_old_events" service/events.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))

fi

if grep -q "def _collect_system_gauges" service/metrics.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _collect_system_gauges preserved in service/metrics.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _collect_system_gauges wrongly removed from service/metrics.py"
fi

if grep -q "def _compact_collection" service/storage.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _compact_collection preserved in service/storage.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _compact_collection wrongly removed from service/storage.py"
fi

if grep -q "def _cleanup_finished_tasks" service/scheduler.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _cleanup_finished_tasks preserved in service/scheduler.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _cleanup_finished_tasks wrongly removed from service/scheduler.py"
fi

if grep -q "def _setup_scheduler_maintenance" service/notifier.py; then
    SCORE=$((SCORE + 1))
    echo "  PASS: _setup_scheduler_maintenance preserved in service/notifier.py"
else
    echo "  FAIL: _setup_scheduler_maintenance wrongly removed from service/notifier.py"
fi

echo ""
echo "Score: $SCORE/$TOTAL"
if [ "$SCORE" -ge 25 ]; then
    exit 0
else
    exit 1
fi
